Title: The transcription factor WRKY41–FLAVONOID 3′-HYDROXYLASE module fine-tunes flavonoid metabolism and cold tolerance in potato

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Histone acetyltransferase 1; contains TAZ and ZZ domains/fragments described
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and freezing tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a coactivator that enhances ScF3′H expression through increased H3K27 acetylation, indirectly promoting flavonoid-mediated cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S used for ScWRKY41 overexpression in hairy root context examining H3K27ac effects
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Directly involved in epigenetic activation by increasing H3K27ac at the ScF3′H promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; AlphaFold3 docking; BiFC; ChIP-qPCR for H3K27ac enrichment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScHAC1 physically interacts with ScWRKY41. 2. Cold increases H3K27ac enrichment at the ScF3′H promoter. 3. ScWRKY41 recruits ScHAC1 to the ScF3′H locus, indirectly activating flavonoid metabolic gene expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential cofactor target to strengthen inducible activation of beneficial flavonoid metabolism, though direct metabolic engineering value is less established than ScWRKY41 or ScF3′H
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as an epigenetic coactivator rather than a pathway enzyme or classical TF

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: StF3′H ortholog; cultivated potato homolog DM8C03G29990
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Desiree; DM1-3 516R44 (DM); also functional knockout tested in S. commersonii CM
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol and anthocyanin branch; B-ring 3′-hydroxylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dihydrokaempferol; kaempferol-related flavonoids; flavanones/flavonoid substrates requiring B-ring 3′-hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dihydroquercetin/quercetin-related flavonoids; eriodictyol-related products; quercetin-O-glycosides; cyanidin-type anthocyanin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the flavonoid B ring at the 3′ position, converting kaempferol-related intermediates toward quercetin-related flavonoids and promoting more highly hydroxylated anthocyanin/flavonol products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of freezing/cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward target product; forms immediate precursors and promotes downstream accumulation of hydroxylated flavonols/anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point to downstream hydroxylated flavonols and anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin/quercetin-derivative accumulation, enhances antioxidant capacity, lowers ROS accumulation, and improves freezing tolerance; knockout impairs freezing tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by cold in S. commersonii (CM)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Unchanged under cold in DM; overexpression in Desiree increases transcript abundance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress/freezing treatment (4 °C induction; -2 °C and -4 °C freezing)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CNCB CRA018559); flavonoid metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/branch-switch enzyme in flavonoid metabolism under cold response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in S. commersonii; overexpression in S. tuberosum cv. Desiree; anthocyanin quantification; flavonoid metabolomics; DAB/NBT staining; ABTS antioxidant assay; electrolyte leakage under freezing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScF3′H is the only tested flavonoid-pathway gene consistently induced by cold in freezing-tolerant S. commersonii CM. 2. Loss of ScF3′H reduces anthocyanin content and impairs freezing tolerance in CM. 3. Overexpression of ScF3′H in cultivated potato increases hydroxylated flavonoids, antioxidant capacity, and freezing tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering cold-tolerant potato by boosting antioxidant flavonoids; can be stacked with its upstream regulator ScWRKY41 for stronger inducible response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScF3′H overexpression reduced kaempferol-O-glycosides and pelargonidin while increasing quercetin-O-glycosides and related anthocyanins, indicating metabolic flux redirection toward more hydroxylated flavonoids

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum commersonii; Solanum tuberosum; Nicotiana benthamiana (transient assays)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group III WRKY transcription factor with WRKY domain and nuclear localization signal
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and chromatin-associated activation via recruitment of histone acetyltransferase
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates ScF3′H transcription by binding W-box cis-elements in its promoter and increasing promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cold-induced upstream activator of ScF3′H that enables accumulation of hydroxylated flavonoids linked to freezing tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible negative feedback in cold response hypothesized in Discussion, not directly proven
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates ScF3′H and thereby promotes flavonoid antioxidant metabolism associated with improved cold/freezing tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by cold in freezing-tolerant S. commersonii genotypes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in S. commersonii genotypes under cold but downregulated or unchanged in DM
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native ScF3′H promoter fragments used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (CNCB CRA018559) used to identify cold-responsive WRKYs
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Recruits ScHAC1 to enhance H3K27ac enrichment at the ScF3′H promoter under cold and upon ScWRKY41 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate screening; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; transient dual-luciferase assay; hairy-root overexpression; ChIP-qPCR; subcellular localization; BiFC; Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScWRKY41 is a cold-induced WRKY that binds the W-box-containing 284-bp cold-responsive region of the ScF3′H promoter. 2. ScWRKY41 activates ScF3′H transcription in transient and hairy-root assays. 3. ScWRKY41 forms homodimers and recruits ScHAC1 to raise H3K27ac at the ScF3′H locus, strengthening cold-responsive flavonoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulatory node for breeding or engineering inducible cold tolerance through activation of flavonoid antioxidant pathways; may be combined with ScF3′H
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScWRKY30 showed weak promoter binding in Y1H but was not pursued as a core regulator
